# @zakkster/lite-raf

> Zero-GC frame-rate scheduling for the @zakkster/lite-signal reactive graph. A single
> requestAnimationFrame loop exposes the frame clock as three reactive signals and provides
> rafEffect(): reactive effects that run at most once per frame and every frame the loop ticks.
> ESM-only. Peer-depends on @zakkster/lite-signal ^1.1.0. MIT. Author: Zahary Shinikchiev.

## Install
npm i @zakkster/lite-raf @zakkster/lite-signal
@zakkster/lite-signal MUST be a peer dependency (single shared default registry). A second nested
copy silently breaks the frame clock because frame signals live in lite-signal's default registry.

## Import
import { rafEffect, frameTime, frameDelta, frameCount, startFrames, stopFrames } from '@zakkster/lite-raf';

## API
- rafEffect(fn: () => void): () => void
  Registers a lite-signal effect scheduled to the frame boundary. Body re-runs at the END of each
  frame in which a tracked dependency changed; at most once per frame; every frame if it reads any
  frame signal. Returns an idempotent dispose function.
- frameTime: ReadonlyFrameSignal — current frame timestamp in ms (DOMHighResTimeStamp), monotonic.
- frameDelta: ReadonlyFrameSignal — ms since previous frame; 0 on first frame; use as animation dt.
- frameCount: ReadonlyFrameSignal — frames since module load; 32-bit, wraps negative after ~414 days @60fps.
- startFrames(): void — start the rAF loop; idempotent; resets delta baseline (first delta = 0).
- stopFrames(): void — stop loop, cancel pending frame; effects are RETAINED and resume on next start.

ReadonlyFrameSignal: callable () => number (tracked read); .peek() => number (untracked);
.subscribe(fn: (n:number)=>void) => (()=>void) unsubscribe. No .set — only the loop drives them.

## Core semantics
- AT MOST ONCE PER FRAME: many synchronous writes to an effect's deps between frames collapse to a
  single run with the final values (lite-signal QUEUED flag set on first schedule, cleared only when
  the effect actually runs at the frame boundary).
- EVERY FRAME (fixed-rate-display safety): the three frame signals are created with equals:()=>false
  so writes ALWAYS propagate even when the numeric value is bit-identical. Without this, a locked
  60Hz display producing identical deltas would short-circuit lite-signal's Object.is equality and
  FREEZE animations. This is intentional and tested.
- ORDERING: loop re-arms rAF, writes (time,delta,count) inside one batch (so dependents see a
  consistent triple and are queued once), lite-signal flush hands each dirty rafEffect to the
  scheduler which parks it in a pre-allocated queue, loop drains the queue at the end of the SAME frame.
- CASCADE LATENCY: a rafEffect that writes a signal another rafEffect reads schedules the downstream
  effect for the NEXT frame (chains lag one frame per link).
- DISPOSAL: a trampoline already queued for the current frame is neutralised by lite-signal's
  generation guard if its effect was disposed first; the body will not run after disposal.
- ERROR ISOLATION: a throwing effect is caught (console.error) and does not abort sibling effects.
- LIFECYCLE: effects created before startFrames() run once on the first started frame with latest values.

## Zero-GC / memory
- Loop core: zero allocations per frame (pre-grown queue array reused forever; per-frame writes via a
  single hoisted applyFrame function; loop re-arms with same reference).
- rafEffect path: one transient, nursery-collected dispatch closure per ACTIVE effect per frame
  (allocated by lite-signal's flush). Never retained.
- Retained heap growth: ~0 (measured 0.025–0.048 bytes/frame over 200k frames, i.e. flat/no leak).

## Performance (Node 22, Linux x64 CI sandbox; run `npm run bench` for your hardware)
- ~1.3e7 effect-dispatches/sec steady state.
- 100 effects: 0.008 ms/frame (0.048% of a 60fps 16.67ms budget).
- 500 effects: 0.039 ms/frame (0.234% of budget).

## Registry note
Binds to lite-signal's default registry (node pool ~1024 nodes by default). For more nodes or
isolated graphs, call setDefaultRegistry(createRegistry({ maxNodes, onCapacityExceeded: 'grow' }))
BEFORE creating rafEffects.

## Example: dt-based animation
import { signal } from '@zakkster/lite-signal';
import { rafEffect, frameDelta, startFrames } from '@zakkster/lite-raf';
const x = signal(0);
rafEffect(() => { x.update(px => px + 120 * frameDelta() / 1000); draw(x.peek()); });
startFrames();

## Example: pause via signal, no loop changes
const playing = signal(true);
rafEffect(() => { if (playing()) step(frameDelta()); render(); });
pauseBtn.onclick = () => playing.update(v => !v);   // loop keeps running; motion stops

## Gotchas
- First frameDelta is 0 — guard divisions: 1000 / Math.max(frameDelta(), 0.001).
- An effect that reads NOTHING reactive runs once (on first frame) and never again — read a frame
  signal to get per-frame cadence.
- Non-browser runtime: provide a requestAnimationFrame/cancelAnimationFrame shim (see test/harness.js).

## Scripts
npm test (14 tests) · npm run test:gc · npm run bench · npm run verify

## Not in scope
Rendering, tweening/easing, scene graph, fixed-timestep loops (run an accumulator inside a rafEffect),
multiple independent clocks (single module singleton loop).
